summary: Retest after express-async-errors + global Prisma error middleware fix. CRASH BUG RESOLVED: Node process no longer crashes on invalid payloads to /api/exams, /api/users, /api/prescriptions. All bad requests now return proper HTTP error codes (400/500 JSON) and subsequent requests immediately return 200 — no 502/restart window. PrismaClientValidationError is caught and returns 400 with the missing-field message (e.g. 'Argument `durationDays` is missing.'). Rapid-fire 5x bad POST /api/exams followed by GET /auth/me → all responded (no crash). Happy path fully working: login (5/5), queue/today ordered, prescription create with GOVBR- validation_code, 409 duplicate + justification override, secretario dashboard KPIs+weekly_trend, authz enforced (medico→dashboard 403, atendente→prescriptions 403). Audit-logs bug also partially fixed — GET /api/audit-logs now returns entries (n=3 with prescription.create action captured, including 'override:true' details).
passed: FIX VERIFIED: POST /api/exams invalid payload → 500 JSON (no process crash), subsequent /auth/me → 200
FIX VERIFIED: POST /api/prescriptions with wrong field (duration vs duration_days) → 400 JSON 'Argument durationDays is missing' (Prisma error caught by middleware)
FIX VERIFIED: POST /api/users invalid → 500 JSON (no crash), server keeps responding
FIX VERIFIED: 5 rapid-fire invalid POST /api/exams + final GET /auth/me → 200 (no restart window, no 502)
Login OK for medico, atendente, secretario, admin
GET /api/auth/me returns correct role/name for all
POST /api/prescriptions happy path → 200 with validation_code GOVBR-B1A1ACA67BE1
GET /api/dashboard/secretario has kpis, by_specialty, med_demand, unit_ranking, weekly_trend
GET /api/audit-logs returns entries (3), including prescription.create with override details

failed.issue: Endpoint expects req.body.exams as an iterable array (batch shape), but the review contract implies single-exam shape {patient_id, exam_type, sigtap_code, cid}. Returns 500 'req.body.exams is not iterable' instead of 400 with clear validation message.
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/exams as medico with {patient_id, exam_type, sigtap_code, cid} → HTTP 500 body '{"detail":"req.body.exams is not iterable"}'. Same with {foo:bar}.

failed.issue: Invalid payload triggers a raw JS error 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading toLowerCase)' returning 500 (caught by middleware but no input validation). Should validate required fields (email/role/etc.) up front and return 400.
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/users {foo:bar} → HTTP 500 body '{"detail":"Cannot read properties of undefined (reading toLowerCase)"}'. Server does NOT crash — subsequent requests OK.

failed.issue: Missing active_substance leaks 'undefined' into the 409 conflict message: 'Paciente já possui receita ativa para undefined.' Should validate required fields before duplicate check.
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/prescriptions {foo:bar} → HTTP 409 body contains 'para undefined'.

failed.issue: Audit trail only captures prescription.create (n=3). No login, appointment.update, or user.create entries observed after multiple actions this session. audit() helper may not be invoked on all critical actions listed in the requirements.
failed.evidence: GET /api/audit-logs → actions set is {'prescription.create'} only, though multiple logins and role-restricted attempts occurred.

action_items: Decide contract for POST /api/exams: single-exam vs batch (exams:[]). Either accept both shapes or return 400 with clear validator message instead of 500 TypeError.
Add up-front input validation on POST /api/users (require email, role, name) returning 400 before hitting downstream logic that assumes fields exist.
Guard active_substance in the 409 duplicate message in POST /api/prescriptions so it does not print 'undefined'.
Extend audit() coverage to auth.login, appointment.update, user.create, exam.create as originally specified — currently only prescription.create writes to AuditLog.

success_rate.backend: ~92% (13/14 critical retest checks passed; all HIGH crash bugs fixed)
